# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  protect_from_forgery with: :exception

  # POST /images/convert
  # params:
  #   file: uploaded image
  #   format: output format (e.g., "png", "jpg", "webp")
  #   size: resize geometry (e.g., "800x800", "800x", "x600")
  #   use: "system" (default) or "backticks"
  def convert
    uploaded = params[:file]
    unless uploaded.respond_to?(:path)
      render plain: "missing file", status: :bad_request
      return
    end

    format = params[:format].to_s
    size   = params[:size].to_s
    use    = params[:use].to_s

    input_path = uploaded.path
    output_path = Rails.root.join("tmp", "converted_#{SecureRandom.hex}.#{format}").to_s

    cmd = "convert #{input_path} -resize #{size} #{output_path}"

    if use == "backticks"
      output = `#{cmd} 2>&1`
      if $?.success?
        send_file output_path, type: "image/#{format}", disposition: "inline"
      else
        render plain: output, status: :unprocessable_entity
      end
    else
      ok = system(cmd)
      if ok
        send_file output_path, type: "image/#{format}", disposition: "inline"
      else
        render plain: "conversion failed", status: :unprocessable_entity
      end
    end
  ensure
    File.delete(output_path) if output_path && File.exist?(output_path)
  end
end
